title: athrixia heterophylla subsp. sessilifolia
intro: Athrixia heterophylla subsp. sessilifolia, also known as Cape thyme or wild thyme, is a shrubby perennial plant native to South Africa, belonging to the Asteraceae family. The plant's leaves and flowers have been traditionally used for their medicinal properties, which include antimicrobial, anti-inflammatory, and antiseptic properties. The health benefits of Athrixia heterophylla subsp. sessilifolia include its ability to boost the immune system, reduce stress and anxiety, and alleviate respiratory issues such as bronchitis and coughs. Athrixia heterophylla subsp. sessilifolia can be prepared as a herbal tea, infused oil, or dried powder for use in traditional medicine and herbal remedies.
